content: Routing hardening follow-up after v0.1.1: reduced unnecessary doglegs by treating same X/Y as valid unless it creates exact endpoint overlap, insufficient spacing, component intersection, or long segment overlap. Endpoint deconfliction is scoped by node side, direct/low-bend routes are preferred before axis-conflict avoidance, and final straightening handles mixed-side endpoint-preserving L routes. Added a local-first UI fixture from the browser report, fixed inward endpoint routes by reselecting source/target ports only when the first or last segment would turn back through its own component, and then tightened endpoint incidence so rendered SVG endpoints must visibly leave/enter component sides perpendicular and outward with a minimum 10px stub. Same-side fallback route candidates now include orthogonal Z routes so side ports do not fake a too-short perpendicular stub. The local-first fixture now asserts clean endpoint stubs at layout level and SVG render validation level. Verification: npm test -- --run test/layout.test.ts test/render.test.ts, npm test -- --run test/edges.test.ts test/layout.test.ts test/render.test.ts, npx tsc --noEmit, npm run build, dist render validation for parser_registry. Existing untracked reports/ left untouched.
